title: AI-Driven Resume Quality & ATS Analysis System
description: Built an end-to-end AI hiring analysis system that ingested resumes, LinkedIn exports, GitHub metadata, and interview transcripts into a PostgreSQL knowledge graph, then scored candidate-to-job alignment using a custom ATS engine powered by sentence-transformers and scikit-learn. Orchestrated a multi-agent LangGraph pipeline across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama models to generate explainable skill gap analyses and targeted resume improvements, with validation logic that strictly grounded all outputs in existing candidate evidence to prevent hallucinated credentials. Delivered the complete system through an interactive Textual terminal UI, with reproducible ETL pipelines and documented prompt engineering practices enabling auditable, evidence-backed hiring decisions.

title: Diginetica Session Conversion Prediction
description: Built a session-level purchase conversion predictor on the Diginetica CIKM Cup 2016 dataset (308K sessions, ~4% conversion rate), benchmarking flat aggregate features with XGBoost against a progression of graph neural network architectures—bipartite GNNs, HGT, and HeteroSAGE—that explicitly model item co-view, query–item, and item–category relationships. Implemented strict temporal train/val/test splits to prevent leakage and evaluated all models using AUPRC across multiple seeds to account for class imbalance and variance. Findings quantified whether cross-session graph structure provides meaningful lift over sequential and tabular baselines for conversion prediction in sparse e-commerce data.

title: Portfolio Live Editor
description: Built an Express.js development tool that proxied a portfolio site and dynamically injected a WYSIWYG editing overlay into each served page, enabling live in-browser edits to the site's core data layer (profile, experiences, and projects). The overlay communicated with a custom REST API that read and wrote directly to the source data file, persisting changes immediately without requiring any build pipeline or recompilation. This eliminated manual file editing during content iteration by centralizing updates through a point-and-click interface layered over the live site.

title: Portfolio
description: Built a personal portfolio site to showcase data science work, experience, and projects as a living professional profile. Architected the site as a flat collection of vanilla HTML, CSS, and ES module JavaScript with no framework or build step, using a single `data/site.js` file as the source of truth that drives all four pages simultaneously. Automated GitHub repository metadata sync via custom Node.js scripts that fetch and merge live repo data into the data layer without overwriting manually authored fields like project descriptions and categories.

title: Real Time ML Feature Store And Quant Research Platform
description: Built a modular quantitative research platform featuring a real-time ML feature store, event-driven backtesting engine, and tax-loss harvesting rules engine using Python, DuckDB, and Feast. The system ingested daily OHLCV data via yfinance, stored features in a Parquet-backed feature store, and applied wash-sale-aware harvest opportunity identification across lot-level position tracking. A mean-reversion classifier served as a gating mechanism for tax-loss harvesting decisions, integrating the ML pipeline directly into the backtesting harness.

title: California Real Estate Price Prediction
description: Built an end-to-end machine learning application that predicts single-family residence prices across California using ensemble models including XGBoost and LightGBM, trained on real estate listing data with features such as location, square footage, and property characteristics. Deployed the solution as an interactive Streamlit web app with address-based search functionality, allowing users to retrieve price predictions in real time. Model performance was evaluated across multiple regressors using scikit-learn pipelines, with the best-performing model selected and serialized via joblib for production serving.

title: eCommerce Session Review Analysis - Predicting Online Shopper Purchase Intent
description: Built classification models using Random Forest and XGBoost on 12,330 e-commerce browsing sessions to predict purchase intent, applying threshold tuning to maximize recall and reduce missed conversions, achieving approximately 90% accuracy. Applied K-Means clustering to segment shoppers into five behavioral archetypes, uncovering conversion rates ranging from 0.4% for bounce-prone visitors to 24.3% for high-intent consumers. Identified product browsing depth and administrative page engagement as the strongest purchase predictors using Python, scikit-learn, pandas, and seaborn.

title: Dsc207miniproject
description: Built a data science project using Python to collect, process, and visualize structured datasets through HTTP requests and pandas-based data manipulation. Applied statistical analysis and visualization techniques with matplotlib and seaborn to identify patterns and communicate findings through interactive Jupyter Notebook workflows. The project demonstrates end-to-end data pipeline skills including data acquisition, cleaning, exploratory analysis, and publication-quality visual reporting.

title: Dsc215
description: Due to limited information about this project's specific content, here is a general description based on what is available: This project completed coursework assignments for DSC215 using Jupyter Notebooks, applying data science techniques to structured datasets. The work involved Python-based data manipulation, statistical analysis, and visualization to address course-defined problem sets and derive quantitative findings.

title: Sleepercelmanual
description: Built a manual, hand-curated version of a sleeper cell detection system using Python, pandas, and NumPy to process and evaluate data through Jupyter Notebooks. Implemented data retrieval via the requests library and applied structured analytical workflows to identify patterns indicative of sleeper cell behavior. The project served as a baseline reference implementation to validate and compare against automated detection approaches.

title: SleeperCel
description: Built a full-stack fantasy football roster assistant that integrates with the Sleeper API to surface real-time league data, rosters, and available players through a React + Vite frontend. Developed a Python data pipeline using pandas and NumPy to process player statistics and support AI-driven roster recommendations. The tool provides fantasy managers with an interactive interface to evaluate roster decisions backed by structured data analysis.

title: DSC 80 Project Notebook
description: Without access to the actual notebook content, I can only describe what's visible from the metadata: This data science project leveraged Python libraries including pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, matplotlib, and Plotly to perform data manipulation, machine learning modeling, and interactive visualization. The project followed a structured data science pipeline encompassing data cleaning, exploratory analysis, feature engineering, and predictive modeling using scikit-learn's machine learning algorithms. title: Bike Traffic in Cambridge Interactive Visualization
description: Built an interactive web visualization of bicycle traffic patterns across Cambridge, Massachusetts using JavaScript, HTML, and CSS. Implemented dynamic filtering and map-based rendering to display bike lane usage and traffic volume across different times and locations. The project enabled users to identify high-traffic cycling corridors and underutilized infrastructure through interactive data-driven visual elements.

title: Desktop Tutorial
description: This project served as an introductory tutorial for using GitHub Desktop, demonstrating the core version control workflow of editing files, saving changes, and committing updates to a repository. The exercise involved modifying a README file and syncing changes through the GitHub Desktop interface. It established foundational skills in local-to-remote repository management using Git-based version control tooling.

title: How Airbnb Tourism was impacted by the San Diego Sunshine
description: A static graph which explores Airbnb bookings and revenue throughout the year in San Diego, California. The graph shows how the number of bookings and revenue changes throughout the year, with the highest peaks in the summer months.

title: Study on the Impact of Estrus on Female Rats Throughout the Day
description: This graph is a static visualization of the body temperature across male and female mice. Used to explore differences in the daily cylce of body temperature while highlighting the impact of female estrus.

title: AllRecipes Rating Predictive Classification Model
description: This machine learning model predicts the ratings of recipes on AllRecipes.com based on features of the recipe. We created a multiclass-classifier using a RandomForest.

title: Study of the Presence of Educational Institutions and Income Throughout California
description: An exploratory data analysis of the presence of educational institutions and income throughout California. The project uses a heatmap to show the distribution of educational institutions and income throughout the state of California.

title: Image Processor
description: Built an image processor that can read in an image, apply a filter to it, and then output the processed image. The image processor can apply filters such as negate, grayscale, and edge detection. This project was built using Python.

title: Asteroid Landfall Simulation
description: Written using pandas in the Jupyter Notebook environment, this project simulates the landfall of asteroids using historical datasets, probabilistic modeling, and bootstrapping.

title: Interactive Visualization of Mice Body Temperature Across Gender
description: An interactive visualization of the body temperature across the gender of mice. The visualization allows users to explore the differences in body temperature while allowing filtering and the altering of scope. Features a tooltip which highlights Mouse ID allowing us to examine indvidual mice along with their estrus cycle( should they be female).
